title: Side-channel & crypto-agility / PQC-readiness gap detector
intent: DETECT side-channel risk and crypto-agility / post-quantum gaps in INGESTED config / inventory / advisory text (quantum-vulnerable algorithms, non-constant-time crypto, missing TEE for sensitive compute, timing-leak-prone patterns). Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces the deterministic boundary in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md.

patterns.desc: Quantum-vulnerable / undersized public-key crypto in use (RSA-1024/2048, weak ECDSA/ECDH curves) without a hybrid-PQC qualifier.
Broken / deprecated hash or MAC primitive (MD5, SHA-1) configured for signing or integrity.
Weak / legacy symmetric cipher or mode (3DES/DES, RC4, ECB, export/NULL ciphers).
Non-constant-time comparison / crypto primitive — power- or timing-analysis exposure.
Sensitive key/compute material handled outside a TEE / confidential-computing enclave (keys in plaintext / no HSM / no enclave).
Explicitly no post-quantum / hybrid-PQC migration plan or crypto-agility for key exchange / signatures.
Obsolete TLS/SSL protocol version permitted (SSLv2/3, TLS 1.0/1.1) — downgrade / padding-oracle timing exposure.
Hardcoded / static IV, nonce, or symmetric key — enables differential / replay and side-channel recovery.

verdict_rule: risky/malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match (flag/inventory/require_crypto_review). False-positive policy: modern PQC-safe / hardened config (e.g. RSA-4096, X25519, AES-256-GCM, SHA-256/384, TLS 1.3, hybrid-PQC enabled, TEE enabled, constant-time) must NOT match (see verify suite).
